description: Clean-slate SEC filing agentic review compiler executed by Ralph one story at a time and administered by Claude. The build is governed by the no-fallback, no-backward-compatibility, no-overengineering, no-overfit, no-patchlike-behavior, always-first-principle doctrine.

userStories.title: Freeze Linkflow GO evidence and Ralph governance
userStories.description: As Claude administering Ralph, I need the specs and handoff to name the committed Linkflow probe run and Ralph execution model before any code is added.
acceptanceCriteria: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-design.md states that execution is Claude-administered Ralph, not /goal

docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-design.md states the no-fallback, no-backward-compatibility, no-overengineering, no-overfit, no-patchlike-behavior, always-first-principle doctrine
docs/superpowers/plans/2026-05-08-agentic-review-compiler-ralph-implementation.md is the active human-readable implementation plan
ralph/prd.json is the active Ralph story list
python3 -m linkflow_probe summarize runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z prints Gate: GO
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 0 (binding plan §4 'Phase 0 - Freeze Provider Evidence And Update Authority'). Verified by reading the four governance artifacts in place: design spec §19 declares Claude-administered Ralph and §3 binds the no-fallback/no-backward-compatibility/no-overengineering/no-overfit/no-patchlike-behavior/always-first-principle doctrine; plan §0-§16 maps Phases 0-12 to US-001..US-013; ralph/prd.json contains 13 stories with priorities 1..13. Commands: `python3 -m linkflow_probe summarize runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z` printed `Gate: GO` and Tier-1 supported across SDK connectivity, reasoning levels (low/medium/high), strict structured output (minimal+nested), single tool call, multi-turn tool loop, tool-loop+final structured output, error/retry taxonomy, bounded concurrency 1/2/4/8, and streaming event shapes; `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `7 passed in 0.13s`. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised. Files touched: .gitignore (gitignore .claude/ralph-loop.local.md and .claude/state/), .claude/ralph-loop.local.md (Ralph state file, gitignored), ralph/prd.json (this US-001 notes/passes update).

userStories.title: Create package skeleton and run kernel
userStories.description: As the compiler runtime, I need a new sec_review_compiler package with deterministic run ids, manifests, atomic artifact writes, and a minimal CLI.
acceptanceCriteria: src/sec_review_compiler package exists with __init__.py, __main__.py, cli.py, config.py, errors.py, and run modules
Run ids validate the shape YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ_<slug>_<8hex>
RunManifest includes run_id, started_at, config_hash, linkflow_probe_run_id, and package_version
Atomic JSON/text writes use temp file plus replace
python3 -m sec_review_compiler init-run --deal-slug synthetic-demo --run-root runs/dev creates a run manifest
tests/test_run_kernel.py covers invalid ids, manifest fields, atomic writes, and CLI init
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 1 (binding plan §5 'Phase 1 - Package Skeleton, CLI, Run Kernel'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/{__init__,__main__,cli,config,errors}.py and run/{__init__,ids,manifest,io}.py; pyproject.toml updated to setuptools src layout (where=['src','.'], pythonpath=['src','.']) with deps duckdb>=1.4.0, openai>=2.31.0, pydantic>=2.13.0, dev extra pytest. RunId regex enforces ^\d{8}T\d{6}Z_[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]{0,62}_[0-9a-f]{8}$ plus real-datetime validation. RunManifest is pydantic v2 BaseModel(frozen=True, extra='forbid') with run_id/started_at/config_hash/linkflow_probe_run_id (default '20260508T123815Z')/package_version (default '0.1.0'). atomic_write_text uses tempfile.mkstemp+os.fsync+os.replace and unlinks the temp on failure. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `30 passed in 0.30s` (7 prior probe + 23 new across TestRunId/TestAtomicIO/TestRunManifest/TestCLI including parametric invalid-id rejection, monkeypatched os.replace failure cleanup, frozen-model assignment rejection, and a subprocess test invoking `python3 -m sec_review_compiler init-run --deal-slug synthetic-demo --run-root <tmp>` with PYTHONPATH=src that verified the manifest file appears under <tmp>/<run_id>/run_manifest.json). Live demonstration: `PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m sec_review_compiler init-run --deal-slug synthetic-demo --run-root runs/dev` produced runs/dev/20260508T132646Z_synthetic-demo_f676c2f0/run_manifest.json with all five required keys (linkflow_probe_run_id='20260508T123815Z' confirmed). Editable install via `python3 -m pip install -e .` is blocked by Homebrew PEP 668 externally-managed Python; not bypassed (no --break-system-packages). PYTHONPATH=src is the standard non-installed invocation; it is not a fallback or shim. Files touched: pyproject.toml, src/sec_review_compiler/{__init__,__main__,cli,config,errors}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/run/{__init__,ids,io,manifest}.py, tests/test_run_kernel.py, ralph/prd.json (this notes/passes update). No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Build filing package and atlas
userStories.description: As the evidence layer, I need immutable filing packages, exact source spans, and atlas records before any agent can propose facts.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/synthetic_filing.txt contains a transaction heading, background section, dated Buyer A paragraphs, a table-like block, and an exhibit marker
FilingPackage stores filing_id, raw_text, raw_sha256, normalized_text, and ordered paragraphs
Paragraph splitting preserves source order and original character offsets
SourceSpan.identity() hashes filing_id, char_start, char_end, and quote_text_hash
Atlas records sections, paragraphs, tables, source_spans, section_candidates, and atlas_warnings
Ambiguous section labels create atlas_warnings instead of silent skips
Missing substantive tender-offer exhibit raises a typed error
tests/test_filing_atlas.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 2 (binding plan §6 'Phase 2 - Filing Package, Source Spans, Atlas'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/filing/{__init__,package,spans,atlas,examples}.py and tests/fixtures/synthetic_filing.txt + tests/test_filing_atlas.py; added MissingTenderOfferExhibitError + FilingPackageError to errors.py. FilingPackage stores filing_id/filing_type/raw_text/raw_sha256/normalized_text/normalized_sha256/paragraphs/exhibits and exposes paragraph_for_offset; paragraph splitter walks raw_text by blank-line separator and preserves exact source offsets so raw_text[p.char_start:p.char_end]==p.text. SourceSpan(frozen,slots) computes evidence_id = sha256(filing_id||char_start||char_end||quote_text_hash) deterministically; quote_text_hash is sha256(quote_text). build_atlas yields filings/exhibits/sections/paragraphs/tables/source_spans/section_candidates/atlas_warnings; duplicate-label sections are recorded *all* (no silent skip), tagged is_ambiguous_label=True, and produce a single ambiguous_section_label warning. build_filing_package raises MissingTenderOfferExhibitError when filing_type is in TENDER_OFFER_FILING_TYPES (SC TO-T/SC TO-I/SC 14D9) and no exhibit is substantive (heuristic 'offer to purchase' or upstream-declared). Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `50 passed in 0.33s` (7 probe + 23 run kernel + 20 new across TestSyntheticFixtureContent/TestFilingPackage/TestTenderOfferExhibitGate/TestSourceSpanIdentity/TestAtlas including paragraph offset exact-slice, distinct evidence ids for same quote at different coords, ambiguous-label warning + non-skipping, MissingTenderOfferExhibitError typed-error assertion, declared substantive exhibit flag, table column/row counts). Encountered and fixed during US-003: stray U+001F bytes injected into the spans.py f-string at write time produced separator-augmented payload; stripped via byte-level rewrite (count=3) to match the spec §7 plain-concatenation contract; verified by re-running pytest. Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/filing/{__init__,package,spans,atlas,examples}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/errors.py, tests/fixtures/synthetic_filing.txt, tests/test_filing_atlas.py, ralph/prd.json. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Add retrieval index and deterministic tools
userStories.description: As an agent support layer, I need deterministic local tools for search, quote verification, parsing, and filing-local actor labels.
acceptanceCriteria: RetrievalIndex supports literal lookup, regex lookup, BM25-style token scoring, section fetch, paragraph fetch, neighborhood fetch, and table fetch
verify_quote returns verbatim_present, positions, paragraph_ids, and ambiguity
parse_date, parse_money, and parse_count return conservative structured values
parse_date('early March 2026') returns an ambiguous structured value rather than a fake exact date
parse_money('$14.00 per share') preserves per-share unit semantics
parse_count('between 20 and 25 parties') returns min 20 and max 25
normalize_actor_label is filing-local only and does not create cross-deal actor pooling
tests/test_retrieval_tools.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 3 (binding plan §7 'Phase 3 - Retrieval Index And Deterministic Tools'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/retrieval/{__init__,index,tools}.py and tests/test_retrieval_tools.py. RetrievalIndex.from_atlas materialises per-filing token caches and BM25 stats (k1=1.5, b=0.75) and exposes literal/regex/bm25 plus get_paragraph/get_section/get_table/neighborhood; the index stores raw_text but never mutates it (regex returns RegexMatch records derived from the original string). verify_quote returns QuoteVerification(verbatim_present, positions, paragraph_ids, ambiguity in {absent,unique,ambiguous_multiple}); duplicate quotes are flagged ambiguous_multiple rather than collapsed. parse_date handles exact (Month D, YYYY → ISO), vague intra-month (early/mid/late MONTH YYYY → ambiguous month-granularity with ambiguity_reason='vague_intra_month:<adverb>'), month+year, quarters (Q1/first quarter), and year-only — all conservative; 'early March 2026' returns year=2026/month=3/day=None/granularity=month/ambiguous=True/ambiguity_reason='vague_intra_month:early'. parse_money preserves per_share unit (regex \bper\s+share\b) and supports scale words (thousand/million/billion/trillion) plus approximation flags; '$14.00 per share' returns Decimal('14.00')/USD/unit=per_share. parse_count handles between-N-and-M, dash ranges, more/fewer than (open bounds), approximate, and exact; 'between 20 and 25 parties' returns min=20/max=25/exact=False/ambiguous=False. normalize_actor_label is filing-local: locality='filing', filing_id pinned per call, no cross-filing pooling — distinct filings produce distinct ActorLabel records even for the same canonical label. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `91 passed in 0.39s` (7 probe + 23 run kernel + 20 filing/atlas + 41 new across TestLiteralLookup/TestRegex/TestBM25/TestFetches/TestVerifyQuote/TestParseDate/TestParseMoney/TestParseCount/TestNormalizeActorLabel including a test that the regex API does not mutate the underlying str object id). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/retrieval/{__init__,index,tools}.py, tests/test_retrieval_tools.py, ralph/prd.json. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Create deal-room DuckDB schema and lifecycle
userStories.description: As the deal-room authority, I need append-oriented tables for claims, evidence, verifier verdicts, coverage, conflicts, human decisions, and canonical rows.
acceptanceCriteria: DuckDB schema defines source_records, claim_attempts, evidence_bindings, normalized_values, verifier_verdicts, verdict_aggregates, coverage_checks, conflicts, human_decisions, canonical_rows, and canonical_row_evidence or documented equivalent normalized tables
Lifecycle states include proposed, binding_failed, bound, verified_confirmed, verified_partial, verified_rejected, escalated, consistent, accepted, and superseded
Partial corrections create new attempt_id values and never mutate original attempts
attempt_id and claim_fingerprint are distinct concepts covered by tests
Duplicate semantic facts from two agents remain separate attempts
Latest-verdict-wins behavior is impossible under lifecycle tests
failed_to_check coverage blocks trusted publication
tests/test_deal_room_schema.py and tests/test_lifecycle.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 4 (binding plan §8 'Phase 4 - Deal-Room DuckDB Schema And Lifecycle'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/store/{__init__,schema,migrations,repository,lifecycle}.py and tests/test_deal_room_schema.py + tests/test_lifecycle.py. schema.py defines 12 DuckDB tables (the 11 required by the spec plus claim_attempt_status_history for append-only state-transition audit), the LIFECYCLE_STATES tuple (proposed, binding_failed, bound, verified_confirmed, verified_partial, verified_rejected, escalated, consistent, accepted, superseded), the TRANSITIONS map (terminal states have empty allowed sets), VERDICT_TYPES (confirm, partial, reject, ambiguous, malformed), and COVERAGE_STATES including failed_to_check. apply_schema is idempotent and asserts the EXPECTED_TABLE_NAMES list is fully present after migration. DealRoomRepository exposes typed inserts (SourceRecord, ClaimAttempt, EvidenceBinding, NormalizedValue, Verdict, CoverageCheck, Conflict, HumanDecision), validate_transition+transition_attempt with append-only status_history rows, and create_correction which always allocates a new attempt_id, leaves the original payload_json untouched, and transitions the original to 'superseded'. lifecycle.aggregate_verdicts is order-independent (the test feeds {confirm,reject} in both [v1,v2] and [v2,v1] orders and asserts both yield 'escalated' — proving latest-verdict-wins is structurally impossible) and aggregate_attempt persists fresh verdict_aggregates rows with AGGREGATION_POLICY_VERSION='v1' and the full input verdict_id list. can_publish_trusted refuses publication when any required CoverageCheck has check_state='failed_to_check'. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `120 passed in 1.08s` (7 probe + 23 run kernel + 20 filing/atlas + 41 retrieval/tools + 8 deal-room schema + 21 lifecycle/aggregation/coverage). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/store/{__init__,schema,migrations,repository,lifecycle}.py, tests/test_deal_room_schema.py, tests/test_lifecycle.py, ralph/prd.json. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Implement Linkflow adapter and tool loop
userStories.description: As the model boundary, I need Linkflow direct SDK calls, strict output schemas, environment-only credentials, and an explicit Python tool loop.
acceptanceCriteria: LinkflowClientConfig reads LINKFLOW_API_KEY, LINKFLOW_BASE_URL, LINKFLOW_MODEL, LINKFLOW_REASONING_EFFORT, and concurrency from environment
Missing LINKFLOW_API_KEY fails before any network client call when live mode is requested
Strict structured output schemas exist for scout maps, extractor claim attempts, verifier verdicts, and consistency checker findings
Tool loop uses explicit conversation history and function_call_output records
Malformed tool arguments fail loudly
Offline fake Linkflow responses support unit tests without credentials
Agent code cannot write directly to DuckDB
tests/test_linkflow_adapter.py and tests/test_tool_loop_offline.py pass
Existing linkflow_probe tests still pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 5 (binding plan §9 'Phase 5 - Linkflow Adapter, Tool Loop, Structured Agent Outputs'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/llm/{__init__,linkflow,schemas,tool_loop}.py and src/sec_review_compiler/agents/{__init__,roles,prompts,outputs}.py plus tests/test_linkflow_adapter.py and tests/test_tool_loop_offline.py; added LinkflowError/MissingLinkflowCredentialsError/InvalidLinkflowConfigError/MalformedToolArgumentsError/ToolDispatchError to errors.py. LinkflowClientConfig.from_env reads LINKFLOW_API_KEY, LINKFLOW_BASE_URL (default https://www.linkflow.run/v1), LINKFLOW_MODEL (default gpt-5.5), LINKFLOW_REASONING_EFFORT (default 'medium'; validated to {low,medium,high} per the probe's capability_matrix.json model_and_reasoning_acceptance evidence — xhigh is rejected because the probe never confirmed it; if the operator wants xhigh they must extend the probe first, which honors the no-fallback doctrine), and LINKFLOW_MAX_CONCURRENCY in [1,8] (probe-validated). require_credentials() and build_responses_client() raise MissingLinkflowCredentialsError before any network client is constructed. llm/schemas.py defines hand-rolled strict JSON schemas (additionalProperties=False, every property in required) for scout_region_map, extractor_claim, verifier_verdict (with proposed_correction_json typed as ['string','null']), and consistency_finding; strict_response_format wraps via linkflow_probe.schemas.strictify_schema so the existing probe sanitizer is the single source of truth. ToolLoop runs sync, maintains an explicit `input` history (developer/user/function_call/function_call_output items per the OpenAI Responses API), parses arguments with json.loads and raises MalformedToolArgumentsError on JSONDecodeError or non-object decoded args, raises ToolDispatchError when the agent calls an unregistered tool, and raises LinkflowError when max_turns is exhausted with no final response. agents/{roles,prompts,outputs} are import-isolated from sec_review_compiler.store and duckdb; the test_agent_isolation suite verifies via importlib walk + source-file grep. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `148 passed in 0.78s` (7 probe + 23 run kernel + 20 filing/atlas + 41 retrieval/tools + 8 deal-room schema + 21 lifecycle + 16 linkflow_adapter + 12 tool_loop_offline). Live smoke test was not re-run for this story; the binding probe at runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z/ already proved Tier-1 capabilities including tool_use_plus_final_structured_output. Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/llm/{__init__,linkflow,schemas,tool_loop}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/agents/{__init__,roles,prompts,outputs}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/errors.py, tests/test_linkflow_adapter.py, tests/test_tool_loop_offline.py, ralph/prd.json. No live Linkflow calls were issued by this story; LINKFLOW_REASONING level not exercised on the wire (offline tests use FakeResponsesClient).

userStories.title: Prove first offline vertical slice
userStories.description: As Claude reviewing Ralph output, I need one synthetic deal to move through atlas, one specialist, evidence binding, verifier, lifecycle aggregation, and review export without live credentials.
acceptanceCriteria: Orchestrator.run_synthetic_vertical_slice(run_dir, filing_path) exists
Synthetic fixture produces at least one confidentiality-agreement claim attempt
Claim quote is bound by deterministic verify_quote
Offline fake verifier is used when Linkflow env vars are absent
Exports include claim_cards.csv, review_queue.csv, human_decisions_template.csv, provider_calls.jsonl, and tool_calls.jsonl
Accepted claim has exact evidence
Rejected claim does not enter canonical eligibility
Partial correction creates a new attempt
All generated artifacts are under runs/<run_id>/<deal_slug>/
tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py and tests/test_exports.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 6 (binding plan §10 'Phase 6 - Orchestrator And First Vertical Slice'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/orchestration/{__init__,verifier,coverage,consistency,orchestrator}.py and src/sec_review_compiler/exports/{__init__,review}.py plus tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py and tests/test_exports.py. Orchestrator(deal_slug=, extractor=, verifier=, consistency_checker=).run_synthetic_vertical_slice(run_dir, filing_path) builds the FilingPackage+Atlas+RetrievalIndex, persists filing_package_manifest.json, creates a fresh DuckDB deal-room, dispatches the offline OfflineConfidentialityExtractor (rule-based regex on 'confidentiality agreement' — explicit dev path called out by the criterion, not a fallback), binds each cited quote via retrieval.tools.verify_quote with binding_status accepted/rejected and binding_error_code in {absent, ambiguous}, calls the injected Verifier.verify, records the verdict, calls repo.aggregate_attempt (no recency rules — order-independent per US-005), and walks the lifecycle: confirmed→verified_confirmed→consistent→accepted; rejected/escalated→escalated; correction_required→verified_partial then create_correction (new attempt_id, supersedes_attempt_id pinned, original payload preserved). compute_initial_coverage_for_slice writes a required timeline_events check and an optional exhibit_substantive_offer check; can_publish_trusted gates publication. Five export files materialise under runs/<run_id>/<deal_slug>/: exports/claim_cards.csv (sorted, deterministic columns), exports/review_queue.csv (escalated/verified_rejected/binding_failed with latest reason), exports/human_decisions_template.csv (header-only template), provider_calls.jsonl, tool_calls.jsonl (offline runs leave the JSONLs empty). Tests cover: NDA attempt produced and bound exactly (raw_text[start:end]==quote), accepted attempts have exact evidence; injected _AlwaysRejectVerifier confirms zero attempts reach 'accepted'/'consistent'; injected _AlwaysPartialVerifier creates a new 'proposed' attempt with supersedes_attempt_id pinned and the original at 'superseded'; all five artifact files exist under deal_dir; review_queue is empty when all confirmed and populated when rejected. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `162 passed in 1.12s` (7 probe + 23 run kernel + 20 filing/atlas + 41 retrieval/tools + 8 deal-room schema + 21 lifecycle + 16 linkflow_adapter + 12 tool_loop_offline + 8 vertical_slice + 6 exports). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/orchestration/{__init__,verifier,coverage,consistency,orchestrator}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/exports/{__init__,review}.py, tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py, tests/test_exports.py, ralph/prd.json. No live Linkflow calls in this story; LINKFLOW_REASONING level was not exercised — the offline path is deterministic by construction.

userStories.title: Add deterministic canonical compiler
userStories.description: As the publication layer, I need accepted source-backed attempts to compile into canonical graph rows with row-evidence links.
acceptanceCriteria: Minimal canonical models exist for deal, filing, source_span, actor, event, event_actor_link, and canonical_row_evidence
Compiler reads accepted attempts, evidence bindings, coverage records, conflicts, and human decisions
Compiler refuses unbound attempts, missing evidence coordinates, failed_to_check coverage, and blocking conflicts
Canonical ids are deterministic from run id, deal slug, source ids, and payload keys
Deleting canonical rows and recompiling produces identical rows
Model-owned source offsets are impossible because compiler reads evidence bindings only
tests/test_canonical_compile.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 7 (binding plan §11 'Phase 7 - Canonical Compiler'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/canonical/{__init__,models,compiler}.py and tests/test_canonical_compile.py. Pydantic models (extra='forbid', frozen=True) for CanonicalDeal/Filing/SourceSpan/Actor/Event/EventActorLink and a separate CanonicalRowEvidenceLink. canonical_row_id(table, run_id, deal_slug, source_ids, payload_keys) hashes the sorted keys/source_ids together so reordered inputs produce identical ids. CanonicalCompiler.compile() applies refusal gates first (coverage_failed_to_check via lifecycle.can_publish_trusted; blocking_conflict for any conflict in resolution_state in {'open','escalated'}) and aborts atomically — no rows are written when refusals fire — then wipes any prior compile output for the run id, fetches accepted attempts and accepted evidence_bindings only, and emits deal + filing + source_span rows from binding coordinates plus event rows for accepted timeline_event attempts with one canonical_row_evidence link per (event,attempt,evidence_id,ordinal). Source offsets always come from evidence_bindings.char_start/char_end (never attempt.payload_json) — proven by a test that injects payload['char_start']=999999/['char_end']=999999 and asserts the compiled source_span payload still uses the binding coordinates. Tests cover: happy compile produces deal=1/filing=1/source_span≥1/event≥1; events have canonical_row_evidence links; recompile-after-delete is bit-identical (canonical_row_id sets equal); canonical_row_id helper is order-independent; rejected-only run yields zero events; injected failed_to_check coverage refuses with code coverage_failed_to_check; injected open conflict refuses with code blocking_conflict. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `171 passed in 1.22s` (162 prior + 9 canonical). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/canonical/{__init__,models,compiler}.py, tests/test_canonical_compile.py, ralph/prd.json. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Add human decision import and coverage ledger
userStories.description: As the hand-review interface, I need human decisions and coverage states represented in the data model without a UI dependency.
acceptanceCriteria: human_decisions_template.csv import supports accept, reject, correct, and defer
Human correct creates a new claim attempt unless the correction is notes-only
Coverage states include checked_found, checked_absent, ambiguous, not_applicable, and failed_to_check
failed_to_check blocks trusted canonical publication
checked_absent remains review-visible but does not invent a fact
tests/test_exports.py, tests/test_lifecycle.py, and tests/test_canonical_compile.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 8 (binding plan §12 'Phase 8 - Human Decision Import And Coverage Ledger'). Created src/sec_review_compiler/exports/human_decisions.py and tests/test_human_decisions.py + tests/test_coverage_states.py; extended src/sec_review_compiler/orchestration/coverage.py and src/sec_review_compiler/exports/__init__.py. parse_human_decisions_csv enforces required columns (attempt_id, decision, reviewer, reviewed_at), rejects unknown decisions and non-ISO timestamps, and treats whitespace-only correction_json/notes as None. apply_human_decisions(repo, decisions=, deal_slug=, transitioned_at=) ALWAYS records a human_decisions row first (audit invariant) then drives lifecycle: accept follows _ACCEPT_PATHS table — e.g. verified_rejected → escalated → accepted; reject follows _REJECT_PATHS — accepted → superseded directly, consistent → escalated → superseded; correct calls repo.create_correction unless correction_json is None/empty (notes-only path records the decision and returns without spawning a new attempt — verified by test_notes_only_correct_does_not_create_attempt); defer just records the decision. The original attempt's payload_json is verified preserved bit-for-bit by test_correct_creates_new_attempt_and_supersedes_original. orchestration.coverage.compute_initial_coverage_for_slice now exercises all five COVERAGE_STATES: checked_found/checked_absent for timeline_events, not_applicable for non-tender exhibit_substantive_offer, failed_to_check for tender filings missing the substantive exhibit (defensive — package builder gates earlier), ambiguous for section_label_resolution when atlas.atlas_warnings has ambiguous_section_label entries; only timeline_events and tender-offer substantive_offer are required. Tests cover: failed_to_check blocks (existing US-008), checked_absent allows compile and does not invent canonical bid rows (test_checked_absent_does_not_invent_a_fact), ambiguous and not_applicable do not block. Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `188 passed in 1.20s` (171 prior + 8 human_decisions + 9 coverage_states). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/exports/{__init__,human_decisions}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/orchestration/coverage.py, src/sec_review_compiler/orchestration/orchestrator.py (passes atlas to coverage builder), tests/test_human_decisions.py, tests/test_coverage_states.py, ralph/prd.json. No agent calls in this story so no LINKFLOW_REASONING level was exercised.

userStories.title: Add full agent role surface
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need the full V1 agent role surface without expanding canonical schema beyond proven vertical-slice needs.
acceptanceCriteria: Roles include scout, party_relation_extractor, timeline_bid_extractor, count_coverage_extractor, omission_inspector, verifier, and consistency_checker
Every role has a strict output schema
Every role has a prompt hash
No role exposes a comparison or answer-key tool
Omission inspector emits coverage checks only, not free-form speculation
No role writes truth directly
tests/test_agent_roles.py and tests/test_orchestrator_vertical_slice.py pass
python3 -m pytest -q passes
userStories.notes: Phase 9 (binding plan §13 'Phase 9 - Full Agent Team Skeleton'). Restructured src/sec_review_compiler/agents/{roles,prompts,outputs,__init__}.py and added src/sec_review_compiler/agents/tool_surface.py and tests/test_agent_roles.py; extended src/sec_review_compiler/llm/{schemas,__init__}.py. AgentRole now contains exactly the seven plan-required roles: scout, party_relation_extractor, timeline_bid_extractor, count_coverage_extractor, omission_inspector, verifier, consistency_checker (verified by test_required_roles_present which asserts the enum values match the required set bit-for-bit). prompts.py defines a unique system prompt per role; role_prompt_hash returns sha256 over the prompt text and test_prompt_hashes_are_distinct_per_role asserts all seven digests are distinct. llm.schemas defines per-role strict JSON schemas (scout_region_map, extractor_batch, omission_inspector_output, verifier_verdict, consistency_findings_batch) and a role_schema(role)/role_response_format(role) registry; agents.outputs.ROLE_OUTPUT_MODEL maps each role to a frozen pydantic model; OmissionInspectorOutput admits only coverage_checks (no prose/notes/description fields — verified by test_omission_schema_has_no_prose_field and test_extra_top_level_keys_rejected). tool_surface.py defines per-role allowlists drawn from a read-tool/list-tool catalogue and a FORBIDDEN_TOOL_NAMES set including compare_to_baseline, lookup_answer_key, internet_search, execute_sql, write_database, write_filesystem; a module-level assert + test_role_does_not_expose_forbidden_tool guards every role. Agents module remains import-isolated from sec_review_compiler.store and duckdb (regression test_no_duckdb_or_store_import + test_source_files_do_not_mention_duckdb). Commands run: `python3 -m pytest -q` reported `234 passed in 1.62s` (188 prior + 46 across TestRoleSurface, TestEveryRoleHasArtifacts, TestNoForbiddenTools, TestOmissionInspectorEmitsCoverageOnly, TestAgentsModuleIsolation). Files touched: src/sec_review_compiler/agents/{roles,prompts,outputs,tool_surface,__init__}.py, src/sec_review_compiler/llm/{schemas,__init__}.py, tests/test_agent_roles.py, ralph/prd.json. No live Linkflow calls in this story; LINKFLOW_REASONING level not exercised — the role surface is structural (schemas, prompts, prompt hashes, allowlists).

userStories.title: Run live synthetic Linkflow vertical slice
userStories.description: As the operator, I need one credential-backed synthetic run to prove the architecture with live Linkflow calls while keeping secrets out of artifacts.
acceptanceCriteria: CLI command python3 -m sec_review_compiler run-synthetic --run-root runs/dev --deal-slug synthetic-demo exists
CLI command python3 -m sec_review_compiler summarize-run runs/dev/<run_id>/synthetic-demo exists
Live mode without credentials fails before network
Live mode with credentials reads them from environment only and never prints them
Live synthetic run creates deal_room.duckdb, review exports, tool logs, provider logs, and canonical output
quality_reports/session_logs/2026-05-08_live_vertical_slice.md records redacted command, run directory, agent call count, claim count, verdict counts, canonical row count, and review queue count
python3 -m pytest -q passes
A targeted secret scan finds no real credential

userStories.title: Add verifier calibration seed
userStories.description: As Claude evaluating verifier rigor, I need a calibration artifact with adversarial cards and fail-closed metrics before trusting verifier output.
acceptanceCriteria: calibration/verifier_seed.jsonl contains at least 12 seed cards
Seed cards include confirmed-correct, single-field errors, multi-field errors, plausible hallucinations, ambiguous cases, and coverage gaps
Calibration runner sends cards through the verifier and computes category metrics
Calibration fails closed if all cards are rubber-stamped confirm
Wrong dates and wrong actors are not counted as confirmed-correct
tests/test_calibration.py passes
python3 -m pytest -q passes

userStories.title: Complete final acceptance audit
userStories.description: As Claude administering Ralph, I need final proof that the clean-slate implementation satisfies the specs, contains no stale authority surface, and leaks no secrets.
acceptanceCriteria: python3 -m pytest -q passes
python3 -m linkflow_probe summarize runs/linkflow-probe/20260508T123815Z prints Gate: GO
Source-tree stale-surface scan finds no live authority references to forbidden prior surfaces outside explicitly allowed archive/probe evidence
Secret scan finds no real credential
git ls-tree inventory contains no .env, .venv, __pycache__, .pytest_cache, .DS_Store, or raw provider secret file

quality_reports/session_logs/2026-05-08_agentic_review_compiler_acceptance.md records exact command outputs, probe run id, live vertical slice run id, known limitations, and next recommended extension
